best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback_long remains the core throughput/PnL engine.
best_edges.evidence: 31 trades, $2411.8 PnL, 252.1 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, 12.0 min median hold; worst MAE -403.2 is material but not the global tail.

best_edges.edge: charm_wall_breakaway_long has convex payoff per trade and strong efficiency despite small sample.
best_edges.evidence: 5 trades, $985.6 PnL, $197.1 avg PnL, 234.4 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, TP 12.0, no slow trades; latest geometry change improved score by +110.6 and worst MAE by +125.35 from run_033 to run_036.

best_edges.edge: breakout_long_1dte is a real fallback revenue stream but should not be tightened directly.
best_edges.evidence: 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades, $1305 PnL, no slow trades; prior direct 1DTE quality gates hurt score by -355 to -594 and reduced net profit by $317 to $558.

best_edges.edge: squeeze_long has clean risk shape but insufficient current profit expansion evidence.
best_edges.evidence: 10 trades, $721 PnL, worst MAE -208.25, no slow trades. Prior squeeze expansion from run_018 to run_021 was strongly positive, but later compression in run_033 hurt score and worsened worst MAE.

best_edges.edge: Fast winner cohort is the portfolio's main quality signature.
best_edges.evidence: 57 fast winners produced $4881.3 with median MAE -29.75 and median hold 5.5 minutes; broad static pruning risks sacrificing the edge.

broken_edges.evidence: 32 trades and $1200.3 PnL, but owns global worst MAE -513.4. Worst tail held 80.0 minutes for only $42.5 and had high ES-VWAP extension 26.69 with weak early progress.
broken_edges.problem: Profitable but tail-heavy; static broad filters overlap protected extended-VWAP winners.

broken_edges.evidence: Direct 1DTE tightening experiments reduced score and net profit while worsening MAE; current 1DTE stream contributes $1305 over 20 trades.
broken_edges.problem: The profile is useful, but direct stricter breakout gates are a failed route.

broken_edges.evidence: Pullback TP/VWAP expansion from run_015 to run_018 added only $44.4 net profit but worsened drawdown by +1.6 pct points, worst MAE by -1499.5, and created one slow >=12h trade.
broken_edges.problem: Broad pullback expansion creates hidden tail risk and capital drag.

broken_edges.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long has 1 trade, $91 PnL, 455 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE -11.7.
broken_edges.problem: Promising but too under-sampled for aggressive optimization.

broken_edges.evidence: Several router-listed profiles are enabled false or produced zero trades in the latest run.
broken_edges.problem: Dead/unproven profiles should not receive parameter tuning without an explicit activation/split experiment.
broken_edges.profile: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long

market_regime_read: This is a short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only regime over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, but hidden MAE remains the binding risk: average MAE about -101.5, worst MAE -513.4, and only 9.5% of trades saw MFE before MAE. Profit expansion should therefore prioritize PnL per exposure hour and convex target capture in proven profiles, while protecting fast winners and avoiding broad filters that merely reduce throughput. The leader is run_029, not the latest run_036; run_036 improved PnL/exposure slightly but lost $64.7 net profit and 2 trades versus run_029.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 1DTE fallback is profitable, but direct breakout tightening failed; a companion split may separate pullback/reclaim behavior without damaging breakout_long_1dte.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap could cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion could be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries with ES pullback/reclaim, setup quality, VWAP distance, and 1DTE option-map fields.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Charm-pin tails differ most in early path behavior, not cleanly in static pre-entry features.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Can overfit a few delayed recoveries or cut delayed but profitable winners.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path features: first +1.5/+2.0 min, MFE before MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, TP-touch timing, and profile context.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension/recent-high-rejection entry that fails to make early positive progress after entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and slow_12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pin-avoid concepts may split high-extension charm/pin risk without globally blocking clean extended-VWAP winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Activation may be unreachable or may cannibalize clean charm_pin_pullback_long/charm_wall_breakaway_long winners.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall MAE, protected fast-winner retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk state currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of run_029 and protected fast-winner retention is not materially degraded.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Call-wall acceptance has excellent first trade efficiency and very low MAE, but needs sample size.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Loosening call-wall gates may admit weak acceptance and dilute the high-quality sample.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Isolated Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance entries, call-volume increase, DEX/charm context, standalone profile attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Strong call-wall acceptance with high setup quality and low MAE behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 total call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=250, worst MAE <= -150, and total net profit >=98% of run_029.

next_experiments.config_bias: Restore/hold the profitable squeeze continuation philosophy rather than compressing exits; keep option-map and order-book confirmation strong and avoid weaker admission expansion unless it is paired with higher continuation quality.
next_experiments.goal: Recover incremental net profit from squeeze continuation without adding tail MAE.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The failed squeeze compression cut convexity; a controlled reversion toward the prior profitable expansion family can improve score and PnL/exposure while preserving the clean -208.25 worst MAE profile.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Convexity Reversion
next_experiments.primary_metric: Total risk-adjusted score and squeeze_long PnL/exposure, with total worst MAE no worse than -513.4 and slow_12h = 0.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample and target changes can underpay the best continuations or admit longer exposure.

next_experiments.config_bias: Continue only small admission-geometry probes around supported charm-wall breakaway conditions; freeze exit compression and keep require-breakout intact.
next_experiments.goal: Build on the latest accepted charm_wall geometry improvement and add high-quality sample count.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The latest run_033 to run_036 geometry move improved score, net profit, drawdown, worst MAE, and PnL/exposure. A second small admission study may capture nearby valid breakaways.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Supported Admission Follow-Through
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=225, total net profit >=98% of run_029, total worst MAE no worse than -513.4.
next_experiments.risk: Five-trade sample is fragile; over-loosening can dilute a convex profile or cannibalize pullback/charm-pin winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use a dormant pin-avoid router activation as an attribution experiment only: one router-list replacement plus minimal same-profile tuning if legal. Do not replace charm_pin_pullback_long.
next_experiments.goal: Find a profile split that captures charm/pin tail states without broad pruning.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The global worst tail is charm_pin_pullback_long and static filters overlap fast extended-VWAP winners; a dormant pin-avoid profile may isolate the risk state more cleanly.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Split Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering may route good trades away from proven profiles; activation may produce no trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not tighten breakout_long_1dte directly. If legal, add/activate a 1DTE companion pullback split and measure cannibalization explicitly.
next_experiments.goal: Expand 1DTE profit while improving 1DTE risk quality.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes reliable PnL, but prior direct tightening failed; a companion split can capture a different 1DTE state without harming breakout fallback.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, total score, breakout_long_1dte cannibalization.
next_experiments.risk: The companion may steal profitable breakout entries or be unreachable.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge only the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection boundary family and validate against protected extended-VWAP winners. Avoid broad setup floors, ES-VWAP caps, or structure floors.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce hidden MAE without sacrificing the fast winner engine.
next_experiments.hypothesis: All 24 bad-MAE rows are covered by the extended-VWAP rejection miss family, but prior single boundary nudge hurt net profit; a narrower profile-aware boundary study may improve MAE with smaller PnL loss.
next_experiments.name: Profile-Aware Extended-VWAP Tail Boundary Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total net profit >=98% of run_029, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, and PnL/exposure flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Even surgical boundaries overlap clean extended-VWAP winners; prior boundary probe lost $80.1 and score.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate stale/no-0DTE fallback behavior only; do not change VALID_0DTE routing or the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner unless the optimizer can separate states.
next_experiments.goal: Improve fallback efficiency and reduce capital drag from stale 0DTE data states.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE contributes only $162.8 over 5 trades with weaker efficiency, while VALID_0DTE and VALID_1DTE_ONLY are the real PnL streams.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total net profit >=98% of run_029.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample; pruning fallback insurance may reduce total PnL more than it improves exposure quality.

research_notes: Use run_029 as the true base/leader. The latest run_036 is informative but not superior: it has fewer trades and lower net profit despite slightly better PnL/exposure. Profit expansion should avoid repeating known failed families: broad pullback TP/VWAP expansion, direct 1DTE tightening, global call-wall loosening, and squeeze target compression. The strongest forward path is profile-specific convexity/sample expansion plus split discovery, not global entry pruning. Any MAE work must explicitly protect the 57 fast winners and 18 extended-VWAP winners.
risk_controls: Require total net profit >=98% of run_029 for exploratory profit-expansion tests.
Require slow_12h_trades remains 0 for all promotions.
Reject experiments that improve PnL/exposure only by removing more than 10% of completed trades unless net profit and score both improve.
For charm-pin or extended-VWAP filters, require >=95% protected fast-winner retention and >=90% charm-pin PnL retention.
Do not promote small-sample profile expansions unless standalone PnL is positive and total worst MAE is no worse than -513.4.
Treat 100% win rate as non-diagnostic; evaluate MAE, hold time, MFE-before-MAE, and PnL per exposure hour.
